data.title: AWF: Stable Fixed Income For Long-Term Investors
data.description:
The AllianceBernstein Global High Income Fund (NYSE:AWF) is a closed-end fund designed to provide investors with income by investing in fixed income securities. AWF pays out an appealing distribution rate of $0.80/share, yielding 7.09% on a forward basis.

About AllianceBernstein Global High Income Fund

AWF was launched on July 28, 1993, by AllianceBernstein as a diversified fixed-income strategy. AWF has roughly $994 million in net assets, with an average of $2.16 million in share value changing hands on a daily basis. AWF is actively managed, adjusting risk throughout the economic cycle; AWF is meant to be held for a long time horizon as an income-oriented investment strategy.

AWF employs a quantitative and fundamental approach to developing the bond portfolio. The strategy invests in both domestic and international bond issuances, inclusive of government and corporate bonds. Issuances within the strategy may be denominated in U.S. dollars, oftentimes called dollar bonds, as well as foreign currencies. As of August 2025, the strategy was primarily invested in USD-denominated issuances.

AWF is broadly diversified across 1,207 individual issuances, with the top 10 holdings making up only 4.8% of the total portfolio weight. AWF is largely diversified across international regions, with U.S. exposure accounting for 66% of the portfolio weight. The strategy is largely diversified across the EU, UK, and Latin America, with some exposure to APAC.

The strategy is largely invested in high-yield bonds, or bonds that are below investment grade. BB-rated bonds make up the majority of exposure, with a portfolio weight of 43.81%. In total, high-yield corporate bonds make up 74.67% of the portfolio, with investment grade accounting for 13.47%.

From a value perspective, AWF has an effective duration of 2.86 years, making the strategy modestly sensitive to interest rate policy. For reference, a higher duration rate indicates more sensitivity to rate changes.

On Sept. 17, 2025, the U.S. Federal Reserve cut the federal funds rate by 25bps to a target range of 4%-4.25%. Accordingly, the Fed is expecting to lower rates by another 50bps by the end of 2025, with an additional 25bps cut in 2026. The rate cut can be beneficial to foreign issuances as the USD may weaken relative to foreign currencies. This can be particularly beneficial to dollar bonds, or foreign issuances denominated in USD. Holding all else equal, foreign dollar bonds should theoretically become cheaper for the foreign issuers if the USD were to weaken relative to the country’s currency, easing exchange rates and making the issuances more serviceable.

Treasury yields were modestly impacted following the rate cut announcement, likely due to the cut being highly anticipated going into the cut. With rate policy being more or less set for the remainder of the year, I do not anticipate substantial movements that will impact bond yields.

One factor that may impact international bonds is U.S. tariff policy. So far, tariffs haven’t hindered growth across the European Bloc. The Wall Street Journal reported that industrial production in the eurozone turned to growth in July 2025. Some suspect that consumer goods in the EU may have been impacted by some front-running earlier in the year, potentially leading to productivity softening later in 2025. One factor that the report noted was the turn in Germany’s fiscal policy towards loosening fiscal rules to boost spending, as referenced by Reuters in March 2025. Accordingly, looser fiscal policy is expected to create “a €500b special fund to boost the country’s infrastructure.” This investment initiative could potentially offset any headwinds that may result from U.S. tariff policies, benefiting local industries.

Overall, I believe AWF may be well-positioned going forward, potentially receiving tailwinds from the shift in U.S. rate policy.

From a fund-level perspective, AWF can be an appealing fixed-income strategy for investors seeking domestic and international, high-yield exposure. The strategy has the flexibility to rotate assets without limit across regions, duration, issuer type, and ratings. This can be appealing to investors that may not want to actively manage their portfolio’s bond exposure.

AWF pays out a monthly distribution rate annualized at $0.80/share, yielding 7.09% on a forward-looking basis. Distributions have been relatively consistent over the years, providing investors with some predictability for financial planning purposes.

AWF is one of the lower-cost fixed-income CEFs on the market with an expense ratio of 100bps. AWF commands a much larger portfolio relative to peers with $994 million in net assets, providing investors with a certain degree of liquidity for trading and rebalancing purposes.

Looking at performance, AWF has historically performed at the midpoint of its peers, though the fund has outperformed looking back at 6-month performance on a total return basis.

Distributions are also relatively moderate relative to peer CEFs. One peer fund, the PGIM Global High Yield Fund (GHY), may provide an alternative for AWF given its strong distribution yield and historical performance. GHY is relatively smaller with $542 million in net assets, though the strategy maintains similar diversification across 875 holdings. GHY pays out a higher distribution rate of $1.26/share, yielding 9.51% on a forward-looking basis.

Risks Related to AWF

AWF is an actively managed, fixed-income strategy with international exposure, providing certain risks to investors, including manager risk, interest rate risk, inflation risk, and currency risk, among others. Given the international exposure, the fund may be exposed to foreign central bank rate policies as well as U.S. rate policy that may influence exchange rates, potentially impacting cash flows to a certain extent. The strategy will largely be dependent on the portfolio management team’s expertise in security selection and tactical positioning.

Final Thoughts

AWF is an appealing fixed-income CEF that can provide investors with current income as a long-term investment. The strategy may be well-positioned for further interest rate cuts in the United States, potentially leading to improved performance going forward. I am recommending AWF with a "Buy" rating; however, investors may consider GHY as an alternative to AWF.

data.title: Growth Ahead For UMH And Flagship In Manufactured Housing
data.description:
Manufactured housing is well positioned with a price point that affords a large surplus for both producer and consumer. The supply of MH has been artificially held down by onerous financial and zoning regulations, which has created a substantial undersupply. This undersupply is beneficial to the incumbents who have the capability to get through the red tape and can sell into proportionally higher demand.

We believe the MH sector will generally outperform, and we see Flagship (OTCPK:MHCUF) and UMH Properties (NYSE:UMH) as the best positioned within it. Let us begin with the fundamental and regulatory factors pointing toward sector level success and then move on to the individual company analysis.

Sector fundamentals

There are multiple concepts that we believe favor forward growth in MH:

  • A low percentage of MH in the overall housing market is a big part of the affordable housing crisis, and the growing concerns are an impetus for loosening regulation.
  • Regulation is loosening federally.
  • Regulation is loosening at the state and local level.
  • A large and growing price disparity between MH and site-built creates a surplus to be split between buyer and supplier.
  • Better financing options will stimulate demand.

Company-level analysis

At the company level, we favor UMH and MHCUF for the following reasons:

  • Geographic advantage in top MH zones
  • Valuation
  • Same-store NOI superiority
  • MH Pure-plays
  • Manufacturers upping supply improves the profit capture rate for community owners.

Let’s dive right in.

MH is a small percentage of housing compared to history

Shipments of manufactured housing have increased significantly in the last 10 years.

However, the volume is still quite low compared to history. In fact, the last 20 years have been the lowest MH building volume in the last 70 years.

At today’s pace of MH shipments, they make up about 10% of the single-family housing market. Looking at the number of site-built housing starts below, we can see that MH used to be as much as 30% of the housing market back in the 70s.

Regulation on MH got really tight both in terms of zoning and financing, which has been a substantial deterrent to volume in the last 20 years.

I posit that such restrictions on the cheapest form of permanent housing have been a massive contributor to the affordable housing crisis. When the percentage of MH in housing moves from 30% to 10%, that is undoubtedly going to have implications for general affordability.

There are articles everywhere about how difficult it is for young people to afford their first home. The average age of first-time homebuyers has gone up considerably, and even for those who can afford it, the cost is so substantial that it is not the wealth-building proposition that homeownership used to be.

Given the crisis, there is growing social and therefore political pressure to do something about affordable housing. Manufactured housing is increasingly seen as the solution.

At a federal level, there are 2 substantial changes that improve the prospects for MH volume:

  1. 100% bonus depreciation on MH as per the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA)
  2. Loosening financing restrictions, making it easier for homebuyers of MH to get FHA loans. Specifically, they have broadened the scope of what qualifies.

States are also loosening MH regulation. The states mentioned in the link are likely just the tip of the iceberg, with general social and political will encouraging other states to follow suit.

The impetus for such a push for manufactured housing is the sheer magnitude of price disparity. MH can still be bought for an average of around $120K, while site-built homes have gotten relatively more expensive, at an average approaching $370K.

Source: Construction Coverage

That $250K gap in price point provides a lot of room for creativity in finding solutions that work for both the consumer and the provider of MH.

Builders are increasingly experimenting with larger and nicer manufactured housing, such as two-story buildings and multitenant units. These larger buildings now qualify for HUD due to recent changes from HUD Secretary Scott Turner.

With higher quality MH available, home buyers do not have to take as much of a sacrifice on living conditions while still getting a massive discount on price point.

The quality of the product can work in tandem with more available financing options to substantially drive demand for MH.

It has previously been difficult to qualify for MH loans, which meant buyers had to have very high down payments relative to property value. The ratio was such that the down payment required for MH was close to that required for site-built homes since site-built homes allow for higher loan-to-value ratios.

With the increasing availability of FHA loans for MH, MH is becoming substantially more affordable for buyers in overall price tag, monthly payment, and down payment. We think MH can capture a significant portion of would-be first-time buyers who have been priced out of the site-built housing market.

For all of the above reasons, we think manufactured housing will be a rapidly growing sector. Let us now turn our attention to which entities within the sector will capture the lion’s share of the profits.

Sector participants

There are 3 big categories of MH industry participants:

  • MH Manufacturers
  • MH financiers (loans and insurance)
  • MH community owners

If we are correct about the overall volume increase of manufactured housing deliveries, each of these industry participants will get a nice revenue boost. Profit sharing between the participants will depend on strategic positioning and relative scarcity.

I posit that community owners will get the better end of the deal.

There is still relatively little land available that is zoned for MH and properly amenitized. This makes community owners the bottleneck and therefore the segment capable of enforcing pricing power.

In contrast, the manufacturing can be scaled up to meet demand, albeit with some delay.

William Boor (CEO of Cavco) noted on the 2Q earnings call (their fiscal Q1) that they are significantly increasing manufacturing volume:

“Over the last several quarters, we've been executing on a plan to push production up where we have the backlog to support it”

Cavco is positioned to perform reasonably well. I suspect margins will stay roughly flat while volume increases for overall proportional earnings growth.

Financiers can also expand volume to meet demand. There have even been new entrants into MH financing, with the MH REITs stepping in to offer financing to their tenants. As such, I suspect MH loans will remain at normal economic margins for an okay return to the companies that offer them.

The bottleneck seems to be properly zoned land in well-managed MH communities. As such, I think the community owners are positioned to capture an outsized share of profits as the sector grows.

To some extent it is already happening, with the REITs showing a capacity to raise rents at a pace that substantially exceeds inflation or GDP.

Same-store NOI growth across the MH REIT sector has been very strong. Equity LifeStyle’s (ELS) SSNOI is charted below.

The other large-cap manufactured housing REIT is Sun Communities (SUI) with its SSNOI charted below.

Each has been averaging about 5% same-store NOI growth for the last decade.

Their strong MH growth is being partially offset by their other businesses, such as RVs and marinas.

If we look instead at the pure-play MH REITs, the growth is closer to 10% annually.

UMH Properties has roughly doubled the same-store NOI growth of its larger peers.

Small-cap, Canadian-domiciled Flagship Communities has not been around as long but is clocking in at 12% average same-store NOI growth.

The pure-play MH REITs are significantly outperforming the larger mixed-asset MH REITs. Despite the growth advantages, the pure plays are actually trading at a large discount to peers.

Valuation

ELS and SUI trade at 22.8X and 21.2X 2025 AFFO, respectively. UMH is available at 15.9X AFFO.

Flagship does not populate on the screener because of its Canadian domicile, but its properties and management are all in the U.S., so it is absolutely a fundamental peer. It is trading at 13.61X AFFO.

In my opinion, the cheaper valuation and higher growth of UMH and MHCUF make them clearly better than SUI or ELS.

Additionally, I think the geographic footprints of UMH and MHCUF are advantaged.

Geographic advantage

These smaller REITs operate in the states that are most attuned to MH.

Flagship’s property map radiates around its Kentucky headquarters.

It so happens that Kentucky is the number 2 state in MH concentration, with fully 31.6% of its single-family housing consisting of MH compared to the national average of about 10%.

UMH’s property map is also concentrated in the eastern and middle USA, which is where most of the darker shaded states on the map above are.

These areas tend to be a bit friendlier to MH development than the coastal regions, which has allowed the REITs to get their land zoned more efficiently.

UMH in particular has a very large bank of land that is already zoned and, in many cases, adjacent to existing communities. This will allow them to build thousands of additional sites at minimal incremental land cost.

Bull thesis summary

UMH and Flagship have among the best organic growth rates in real estate, yet are trading at discounted valuations. We see the growth continuing for the foreseeable future due to the aforementioned macro and regulatory factors favoring the manufactured housing sector.

Since indexes primarily go by market cap, these companies are virtually nonexistent in any real estate ETFs. As such, real estate investors are woefully underexposed to what is and has been the fastest-growing real estate sector. We are substantially overweight UMH and MHCUF.

Risks to thesis

Aside from the normal equity investing risks, I think the most relevant risk to this article is the potential for the regulatory pendulum to swing too far.

A little bit of red tape loosening is great for the industry. If it gets too loose, however, it could introduce an oversupply threat. Part of the ample pricing power of MH communities is the scarcity of high-quality, safe, and well-amenitized MH communities. If the red tape gets removed to a point where it becomes fast and easy to set up a new MH community, it opens the door for some very well-capitalized players to flood the market.

I think regulation overall is still fairly tight, so I am not worried about this today, but it is a factor to watch.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: SIXG: Strong Buy In The Connectivity Sector
data.description:
Introduction

The Defiance Connective Technologies ETF (NASDAQ:SIXG) aims to invest funds into the next generation of connection and telecommunications hardware and software, especially those that have use cases in AI and advanced computing. The fund looks to invest in companies that are powering the next computing revolution, who need the best and fastest connections to ensure their products and platforms operate at the highest efficiency. Please see below for some key facts of the fund, taken from the prospectus.

Their top holdings are as follows.

We are overall very bullish on the investment thesis of this ETF, as we believe that enhanced telecommunications hardware and software will be incredibly important in this new age of computing. In the following section, we will review some high level trends and news that further our bullishness in the sector.

Industry Trends and News

The 6G wireless market is projected to grow rapidly

The next generation of wireless connective technology is 6G, which replaces the current standard of 5G. Estimates show that the overall market for this technology is forecasted to grow exponentially over the next decade, per Root Analysis.

We believe that the majority of this shift and growth will be driven by the needs of all of these companies and governments who are building the latest and greatest AI models and networks - these require significant amounts of connective power to ensure the models run smoothly and efficiently from a power basis. We believe that this overall trend will still continue, and that as antiquated 4G and 5G networks become more and more obsolete, the 6G market will turn from a cutting-edge use case to a mainstream product - and that will fuel additional growth in the market. This is a powerful factor that would spur investments into the companies in this ETF.

AI investments are growing globally

The overall AI infrastructure market and overarching investment theme that has dominated the markets are set to continue further, as a recent report shows that Microsoft, Nvidia, and other tech giants are planning a massive $40B investment into AI related enterprises in the United Kingdom. This multi-corporate investment affair is also coming side by side with the Trump administration’s visit to the UK, and if prior international travel for Trump is any insight, it could mean that more deals are to come. We believe that this is positive for a few reasons. Firstly, it puts out the fire that has been brewing regarding the ‘AI Bubble’ for the time being - this shows that investment is very much still ongoing and investors are not done putting money into AI-related companies. Secondly, this shows that the governments around the world are still investing in building out their AI portfolios and positioning, and this is another large corner of the market that should bode well for firms in this ETF. Ultimately, we view these continued investments in the AI space as very bullish for this ETF.

Data centers are being built out consistently, for AI and non-AI use cases

Many of the top names in this fund are suppliers of chips, networking gear, cables, connection points, energy, and server racks for data centers - both in the edge computing and AI markets as well as other computing needs for various clients. Overall, we are seeing trends that show data center growth and investment are not stopping - and this is beneficial for the names in the fund. For example, BlackRock announced an investment of $678M in the UK for data centers, Google revealed a $5B investment in the UK for data center related activities, and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is exploring partnerships with private capital firms to fund its massive data center expansion efforts. These instances of investment into the infrastructure needed for the computing of tomorrow are all signs that show these trends are not stopping, and that there is consistent money flowing into this sector - which is fantastic for the names in SIXG. We believe that these trends will continue for the foreseeable future, and with a rate cut on the horizon, borrowing money to fund expansion efforts will only get cheaper. This is a very bullish sign for the companies in this ETF.

Peer Comparison

As we discussed, we are very bullish on the outlook of 5G/6G technologies and telecom growth in general. However, that does not mean that SIXG is the best way to invest in these trends. We will look at SIXG against other ETFs that have a similar theme or holdings:

  • Pacer Data and Digital Revolution ETF (TRFK)

  • SPDR S&P Telecom ETF (XTL)

  • iShares U.S. Telecommunications ETF (IYZ)

We acknowledge that these ETFs do not exactly follow the theme of SIXT, but they have overlap in the holdings and/or macro factors that impact these ETFs. TRFK is the ETF that is most similar to SIXG in terms of holdings with 34.6% overlap with SIXG.

First we look at performance:

We see that in the past 3 years, there is quite a difference in the performance of the 4 ETFs. It is hard to visually see the difference in the chart, so we pulled the numbers out into a table:

ETF

3 Year Performance

Diff From Previous

We see that as we go from the lowest-performing to the highest-performing ETF, there are large gaps in performance, with at least a 41% gap between each one. Of course, TRFK stands out from the pack, as is also evident in the chart. SIXG is in 2nd place with almost 100% gain in the last 3 years.

Next we look at the expense ratios as reported by Seeking Alpha:

We see that the 4 ETFs generally have similar expense ratios, but TRFK stands out again as the highest one and with the largest difference from the next highest. SIXG has the lowest expense.

We now look at the 4-year average dividend yields of the ETFs:

There is a wider gap in yields for the 4 ETFs than we saw with expense ratios. IYZ wins this one with 2.39%, and SIXG is in 2nd place with 1.32%. TRFK does not have a dividend.

Overall, this comparison looks good for SIXG. It is in 1st or 2nd place across all of the metrics we looked at, and we especially like the (relatively) low expense ratio. The high performance over the past 3 years is promising for the telecommunications strategy as well. One concern could be that they have one of the best price performances of the group, so they may be overvalued. However, as we discussed, there look to be a lot of additional growth opportunities in the sector, so we are not concerned about the fund’s ability to perform in the next few years.

Risks to the thesis

AI Investment trends stop

There is a possibility that the AI investment cycle dries up, or investors rotate away from the AI trade. This would be highly detrimental to this ETF - but as we have shown above, from all the news and trends that we are analyzing and tracking, it seems that the opposite is the case. AI investments are still happening across the globe, and forecasts estimate more and more capital deployment into the sector over the next decade as the future of computing gets funded and built out.

Low Trading Volume

In the past 3 months, SIXG has had an average daily volume of 61.16k which is generally considered to be illiquid. This means that if you were to buy the fund, it may be difficult to sell your shares in the future at a good price. One positive side is that the fund’s value comes primarily from its holdings which are generally large, very liquid companies, so the risk of a bad valuation of your shares is lower. However, keep in mind that it may take longer than expected to close any position in SIXG due to its low volume.

Conclusion

To wrap up, we believe in the overall goal of this ETF. The names that this fund holds are also leaders in the AI, advanced computing, telecommunications, and connectivity spaces. They all make exceptional products that are critical to the AI revolution that is happening currently. We view this overall market and industry as a very strong investment opportunity over the next decade. This fund should do well as the overall industry grows, invests more, and scales expansion efforts further.

Furthermore, we see that in comparison to similar funds, SIXG looks quite attractive. It has the lowest expense ratio of the funds we looked at and one of the best performances and dividend yields. We see that not only is the 5G/6G telecommunications strategy effective (and we believe it will continue to be effective in the near future), but also the SIXG stands out from other ETFs employing a similar strategy.

As such, we rate SIXG as a strong buy.

data.title: The Stage Is Set For A Sizeable Equity Market Pullback
data.description:
The stage is set for a potentially sizeable equity market pullback.

Friday will be quarterly opex, and that should help to release the stock market from its current malaise of tight daily trading ranges. This has resulted in realized volatility levels falling to very low levels, with even three-month realized volatility at levels not seen since July 2024.

On top of all this, liquidity levels have fallen sharply, with the treasury general account now back to $850 billion. It has likely resulted in reserve balances held at the Fed falling to around the $3 trillion level, although the number will need to be confirmed following the release of the weekly SOMA report after the close of trading on Thursday, September 18.

When combining the options expiration with declining levels of liquidity in the overall market, it could create a window for not only market volatility to develop but also a case for a sizeable drawdown in the overall stock market.

Gamma Release

Gamma levels in the S&P 500 (SP500) are quite elevated, and that has caused the S&P 500 to pin around the 6,600 strike price. However, once we pass Friday, gamma levels in the index should fall, resulting in the market starting to move more freely.

When the market is in positive gamma, it means that market makers are buyers of the index when it falls and sellers of the index when it rises. Gamma levels are highest right now at 6,600, with overall gamma levels at almost $52 billion per 1% move. However, after Friday, that gamma amount will drop to just $32 billion, a 1% move, and that means the pinning effect at 6,600 will not be as great. Unfortunately, it doesn't tell us which way the market could move, because it could move up just as easily as it could move down.

Even though volatility risk is asymmetric, we know that any move higher in the index will have to come as a grind, given that realized volatility is so low. Currently, 9-day vol is at 5.3, 21-day vol is at 8.6, and 63-day vol is at 8.9. When applying the rule of 16 to these values, it means that anytime the S&P 500 moves above 33 bps, the 9-day realized volatility will start rising, and that sends the entire volatility complex into motion, meaning that implied volatility starts to rise. We know that once implied volatility starts rising, it tends to lead to a pullback in the market.

Liquidity

At the same time, we have seen the Treasury General Account reach $850 billion as the reverse repo facility has fallen to below $20 billion. That would mean that overall reserve balances are likely around $3 trillion based on estimates, versus last week's value of around $3.15 trillion.

To this point, there has been no fallout to risk assets from the decline in reserve balances, but that may still be coming, especially if there is a lag in the time it takes to transmit the changes in reserves to the changes in factors like margin and leverage.

With options expiration on September 19 and liquidity levels greatly reduced, it sets up the potential for increasing volatility and a sizeable equity market pullback.

data.title: RLX Technology: Turning Positive On M&A And New Products (Rating Upgrade)
data.description:
I've become bullish on RLX Technology (NYSE:RLX). It has taken steps to realize its growth potential in new geographical markets and product segments.

My prior August 21, 2024, article touched on the mixed read-throughs from RLX's 2Q24 disclosures.

Positive On Inorganic Expansion

At its 2Q25 briefing on Aug. 22, RLX highlighted the "consolidation of our recently acquired European e-vapor company in June." My take is that this is a wise move. Its new subsidiary is "UK vape retailer Totally Wicked," or "TW," according to ECigIntelligence's research.

Ispire (ISPR) mentioned at its May '25 analyst call that "the UK led the European market by banning (e-cigarette) disposables." The expectation is that the rest of "EU would follow suit in due course," as per ISPR's comments.

TW's addition ensures that RLX remains relevant in an evolving regulatory environment.

The firm acknowledged at the second-quarter results meeting that "disposable products have historically been an integral part of our portfolio." But it noted that "we recognize that the future lies in more sustainable solutions." Its new daughter company's "full suite of capabilities and services in the local market" was also emphasized.

TW boasts a wide assortment of e-cigarettes, including vape kits that come with rechargeable batteries (to substitute for disposables). In my opinion, the acquiree will aid RLX's product mix optimization efforts. Separately, the United Kingdom can be the group's new growth engine.

Euromonitor published a report (not publicly available) titled "Smokeless Tobacco, E-Vapour Products and Heated Tobacco in the United Kingdom" in June 2025. TW's share of the sizeable $4 billion UK vape market is a mid-single-digit percent, as per this research organization's estimates.

RLX began venturing outside its home market, the PRC, in '23.

Non-Chinese operations only contributed slightly over a quarter of its top line last year. There's room for the enterprise to increase its overseas presence.

In the past, RLX's U.K. e-cigs were mainly sold via e-commerce sites or wholesalers.

TW has a network of 100+ physical outlets. The additional distribution channel is likely to translate into higher sales in this country. I also think it's a big difference to benefit from on-the-ground learnings regarding smokers' preferences.

RLX is exploring the possibility of entering "additional European countries" next year based on management's remarks at the Q2 call. The newly acquired UK business should act as a launchpad for its ambitions in this continent.

Potential Product Launches Have Huge Promise

The company is diversifying beyond e-vapor. It revealed at the late-Aug. earnings call that the "2B (To-Business) prototypes" for its "modern oral nicotine products (ONPs)" are done. The next step is introducing these items to "the 2C (To-Consumer) market." RLX's proposal could substantially widen its "Total Addressable Market," or "TAM."

UBS Group (UBS) issued a research note (not accessible in public) with the title "Global Tobacco: US Nicotine Survey" dated Sept. 5, 2025. The bank forecasts that sales of "nicotine pouches," a common form of oral nicotine, will rise +122% in the next five years to $10 billion in the United States by the end of this decade; this category is also predicted to account for a high-teens percentage of American cigarette consumption in volume terms.

My view is that these products should have favorable prospects around the world, because they cater to the needs of specific user segments.

RLX's Q2 analyst call commentary stressed that "oral nicotine fits seamlessly into the lifestyle of office workers and frequent travelers."

Most people have the experience of struggling to squeeze everything into suitcases as they pack for trips. You would assume that smokers prefer sachet-like nicotine pouches over vaping devices and related refillables in that kind of scenario.

On the other hand, white-collar workers have to be mindful of how they are judged by their bosses and colleagues, who might be non-smokers. ONPs can be easily consumed at the workstation, as they are smoke-free and don't emit any odors.

Also, "nicotine pouch users are (on average) 10 years younger than cigarette users," as indicated by UBS's early-September publication cited above. The financial institution's analysis found that these are perceived to be "less harmful nicotine products" by this demographic due to the absence of tobacco.

I see RLX's growth runway being extended with its entry into the ONP space.

You Should Consider These Variant Views

There may be post-M&A integration issues relating to the "Totally Wicked" deal. If RLX doesn't work well with TW's management, its future UK market performance might fall short of expectations.

RLX could take a longer-than-anticipated period of time to market its new ONP-related items. It is also possible that people choose to stay with tried-and-tested oral nicotine brands.

My Closing Thoughts

RLX's rating is revised from a "Hold" to a "Buy."

The group's outlook has become better, considering its recent acquisition and new product roadmap.

My fair price-to-sales valuation for RLX is 7.7 times by using its domestic peer Smoore (OTCPK:SMORF) (6969.HK) as a benchmark. This translates into a 48% (7.7/5.2) upside, taking into account the stock's current 5.2x P/S.

data.title: India Gold Market Update: Encouraging Start To Seasonal Demand
data.description:
Gold resumes climb

Gold prices saw a sharp upswing in late August, with the rally accelerating through the first half of September to hit fresh all-time highs, pushing year-to-date gains to 40%.1 International gold prices climbed 4% in August and added another 6.7% in early September2 as bullish sentiment deepened. Our Gold Return Attribution Model (GRAM) attributes August's gains to a weaker US dollar, elevated geopolitical tensions, and strong inflows into global gold ETFs. The continued momentum in September was supported by positive investor positioning – evidenced by rising futures net longs and sustained ETF inflows. Lower US Treasury yields, amid growing expectations of a Fed rate cut and concerns around the Fed’s independence, have provided additional tailwinds.

Chart 1: Sharp rally in gold prices

End of month LBMA Price PM and domestic spot price changes and movement*

Gold prices in India have closely mirrored international movements, with a weaker rupee (down 3% y-t-d) magnifying domestic price gains. As of mid-September, prices had risen 7.0% month-to-date3 to INR 106,863/10g, bringing the y-t-d increase to 44%. Notably, signs of demand interest have helped narrow domestic price discounts,4 which briefly flipped to a marginal premium in late August and again in mid-September. This marks a noteworthy change, as domestic gold prices had been trading at a near-sustained discount since December (Chart 2).

Chart 2: Domestic gold price discount narrows

NCDEX gold premium/discount relative to the international price*

Festive gold demand emerges, led by investment buying

Gold demand in India is showing signs of a pickup with the onset of the festive season, led primarily by a surge in physical investment demand for bars and coins, according to market reports and anecdotal evidence from trade channels. Investment interest is reportedly outpacing jewellery purchases, as consumers are drawn in by the renewed uptrend in prices and expectations of further increases.

Conversations with jewellery manufacturers and retailers suggest that jewellery demand, while present, remains uneven. High-value, wedding-related purchases have begun and are holding steady, while high prices have dampened lower-ticket daily-wear and discretionary buying, prompting a shift to lower carat products. Large retailers are reporting higher footfalls, supported by aggressive marketing and promotional campaigns,5 along with plans for new store openings. Smaller retailers, in contrast, continue to face muted demand. Robust exchange activity, where old gold jewellery is traded in for new, has also been a key contributor to overall sales. While volumes remain lower y/y, sales value has risen aided by the higher gold prices.

Bullion dealers have reportedly stepped up purchases since early September, likely in anticipation of stronger seasonal demand and a potential boost from increased consumer spending from the forthcoming reduction in Goods and Services Tax (GST).6 The tax cut on a range of items, including consumer goods, durables, and automobiles, takes effect from 22 September, just as the festive season enters its peak following a 16-day inauspicious period.7

Overall, expectations for a strong festive season are building across the gold trade.

ETFs: Sustained inflows and investor momentum

India’s gold ETFs saw a notable surge in inflows in August, marking the fourth consecutive month of positive growth. Cumulative net inflows totalled INR21.9bn (US$250mn), up 74% m/m, aligning closely with our earlier estimate.8 This surge is the second highest of the year, since January.9 The increase is likely driven by the sustained demand for safe-haven assets by investors seeking stability amid weak domestic equities and persistent global trade and geopolitical risks. Initial observations point to a continuation of this momentum, with strong positive net inflows during the first two weeks of September.10

Redemptions during the month were the lowest in seven months, totalling INR1.5bn (US$17mn) - a significant decline from the average redemption of INR7.5bn (US$83mn) recorded in the first seven months of the year. This drop in redemptions suggests that investors are adopting a longer-term perspective, choosing to stay invested rather than cashing out, despite the recent price gains. The total assets under management (AUM) in gold ETFs in India reached record highs of INR724bn (US$8.3bn), with holdings increasing by 2.1t to a total of 70t.

Along with strong inflows, investor participation also saw a significant increase, with 164 thousand new accounts (folios) added, pushing the total number of active folios to 8.03mn, a 24% increase since the start of the year.

Furthermore, a new gold ETF was launched in August,11 bringing the total number of gold ETFs listed in India to 22.12

Chart 3: Inflows gained pace in August

Monthly gold ETF flows in INRbn, and total holdings in tonnes*

RBI stays on hold

The RBI’s gold purchases have moderated in 2025, with no additions for the second straight month in August. Gold was bought in only three of the first eight months of the year, compared with near-consistent monthly additions through 2024. The scale of buying has also slowed sharply, with cumulative purchases at just 3.8t between January and August 2025, against 45.4t in the same period last year.

Despite this moderation, India’s gold reserves have climbed to a record 880t. While volumes rose by a modest 4% y/y, the valuation gains have been pronounced, with the value of the holdings up nearly 40%, on the back of higher gold prices. Gold now accounts for 12.5% of India’s foreign exchange reserves as of end August, up from 9% a year earlier. This highlights how price appreciation has significantly strengthened reserves, even amid restrained buying.

Gold imports rise signals resilient demand

India’s gold imports rose sharply in August, reaching a nine-month high and marking the second consecutive month of high imports, underscoring resilient domestic demand. Imports during the month totalled US$5.2bn, a 37% m/m increase, reflecting seasonal buying interest despite elevated prices. We estimate import volumes of 60-65t, up from 46t in July.

Chart 4: Imports rise to a multi-month high in August

Monthly gold imports in tonnes and US$bn*

Footnotes

1 Based on the LBMA Gold Price PM as of 15 September 2025.

2 1-15 September 2025.

3 Based on MCX Spot gold price PM as of 15 September 2025

4 The premium/discount of local gold prices is based on the LBMA Gold Price AM adjusted for import taxes and exchange rate, which is also referred to as the ‘landed price’

5 Jewellery brands boost festive advertising spends: Storyboard18.com, 05 September 2025.

6 GST council approves rates rationalisation: Ministry of Finance, 03 September 2025.

7 Pitru Paksha 2025 start and end date: Times of India, 09 September 2025.

8 WGC’s preliminary estimate, based on partial data, indicated net fund inflows of INR2,184cr (US$233mn).

9 Net fund inflows in January 2025 of Indian gold ETFs totalled INR3751cr (US$435mn).

10 Based on World Gold Council’s estimates of fund-wise net inflows.

11Angel One Gold ETF was launched in August 2025.

12A full list of the physically-backed gold ETFs we tracked can be found in: Gold ETF: Stock, Holdings and Flows | World Gold Council
